title: [TUI] Parity: restore tui-work chat/tool rendering parity with main
description: Close behavior gaps between crates/tui branch and main, starting with tool result display loss in scrollback and formatting mismatches in chat history. Include clippy cleanup for touched files and validation via cargo test.

logs.msg: Implemented parity pass: restored src/tui/chat_renderer.rs from main and wired IonApp scrollback generation through ChatRenderer::build_lines for stable and streaming agent entries (src/tui/ion_app.rs). This restores main-style formatting semantics for user/assistant/tool/system output and fixes dropped tool-result content.

Added regression coverage: src/tui/ion_app.rs test validates tool result text is preserved in rendered scrollback lines; src/ui/conversation.rs test asserts assistant bullet prefix semantics. Validation: cargo test -q now 524 passing on tui-work.

User-reported regression repro (2026-03-04): scroll wheel in inline mode non-functional and bottom UI perceived broken. Root cause: mouse capture enabled while IonMsg::ScrollUp/ScrollDown are no-ops, so wheel events are swallowed; bottom UI lacked explicit progress row and had fixed configured inline area assumptions.

Fixed inline-height anchor bug: crates/tui/src/terminal.rs now updates start_row/content_cursor when switching Inline->Inline with a new height. This was the likely cause of the bottom prompt region staying visually wrong after dynamic height changes.
Removed duplicate gap after startup header by only inserting blank separators between chat entries, not between the header block and the first entry. Validation: cargo test -q passes (524).

Restored old-style footer semantics in IonApp: prompt-aware composer with › prefix and continuation indent, bordered input box, wrapped visual-height calculation, completion summary in progress row, and richer status row derived from inner state. Validation: cargo test -q passes (524).

User repro (2026-03-10): Shift+Enter multiline composer leaves stale border/text in expanded rows; likely row-clearing regression in IonApp custom composer renderer. Also requested thinner token usage indicator in footer.

User repro (2026-03-10): repeated Shift+Enter can panic in IonApp footer render with y out of bounds at src/tui/ion_app.rs:1220. Likely absolute-vs-local buffer coordinates in custom footer Canvas closures, plus stale row clearing needed.

Fixed multiline footer regression in IonApp by rendering footer canvases with local buffer coordinates and clearing rows before redraw; this removes stale border artifacts and the y-out-of-bounds panic on repeated Shift+Enter. Also changed token bar glyphs from block chars to thin line/dot style and guarded panic-hook restoration during unwind. Validation: cargo test -q passes (524).

Created explicit follow-on subtasks under the parity umbrella: tk-s2ib (crates/tui coordinate/layout audit), tk-ajlv (inline/footer reserve contract), tk-9yt1 (PTY parity checklist). tk-nxz3 remains the ACP architecture track. Current implementation work is focused on replacing the fixed 10-row reserve hack with a stateful reserve that grows with the current draft and avoids footer overflow.

User validation on 2026-03-11: footer reserve contract partially improved shrink behavior, but multiline growth still duplicates border/prompt lines when expanding initially. Text entry box remains the main unstable area in tui-work; broad audit still required before parity can be claimed.

Implemented runtime/footer hardening on 2026-03-11: Terminal now tracks and clears stale inline regions across Inline->Inline growth and resize, AppRunner invalidates prev_buf when render_area changes, and IonApp footer now renders from one FooterViewModel/FooterLayout instead of stacked ad hoc canvases. Added unit coverage for inline-region union math and footer layout/rendering. Validation: cargo test -q now 527 passing.

Committed carried-over Rust TUI cleanup batch onto tui-work before continuing the Go rewrite branch. This keeps the branch stack clean and prevents the Go rewrite from masking unrelated worktree edits.
